cases.label: simple accessible-name change
visibleAcceptanceCriteria: Give the existing star watch control the accessible name Watch incident.
Preserve the existing watch toggle and live-status behavior.
Do not require an unchanged JavaScript companion edit.

cases.label: moderate-safe client-only details-state behavior
visibleAcceptanceCriteria: Opening response details makes the panel visible and sets aria-expanded to true.
Closing response details hides the panel and sets aria-expanded to false.
Preserve the watch behavior and run candidate-generated unittest coverage independently.
Treat the requested behavior as client-only; generated-test prose must not invent backend work.

cases.label: complex export behavior
visibleAcceptanceCriteria: Repeated requests for one incident reuse that incident's oldest open queued or processing job, while different incidents receive different jobs.
The worker resumes the oldest queued or processing job and completes the visible safe export contract.
The export includes only the visible allowlisted incident and timeline fields and excludes the visibly forbidden sensitive fields.
Keep status lookup working and run candidate-generated unittest coverage independently.
Python test declarations must not be treated as application calls.

predeclaredSuccessCriteria: All three automatic routes exactly match the expected model and reasoning effort.
Generated tests and verification prose invent no production layer.
No repair is driven by any removed false diagnostic listed in forbiddenRepairDiagnostics.
The visible acceptance contract and hidden verification both pass.
Hidden oracle paths, commands, contents, and failure details never enter model-visible generation or repair context.
Observed cost, outstanding reservations, and committed cost are persisted for every attempted case.
Committed cost never exceeds 3 USD and reservations use the 49152-token transport minimum.
forbiddenRepairDiagnostics: An unchanged JavaScript companion is treated as a mandatory edit.
Client-only interaction is treated as backend work.
Generated-test wording is treated as full-stack production work.
Python test declarations are treated as application calls.
integrityStopRules: Do not rerun any failure, timeout, refusal, or poor model response.
Stop the batch immediately if hidden-oracle exposure or another evaluation-integrity violation appears.
Retain and label an integrity-violating run invalid; make no further paid call.
After paid execution, do not change production code, prompts, fixtures, labels, routes, acceptance criteria, pricing, or oracles to improve the result.
requiredReporting: Every raw and final result, including failures and interruptions.
Route accuracy, raw pass@1, repair-assisted success, and generated-test independent execution.
Model, validator, repair, oracle, provider, and budget failure classification.
Observed cost, outstanding reservations, committed cost, latency, retries, and unknown canceled-call exposure.
